title: 11 Herbal Creams For Gastroenteritis
intro_desc: Herbal creams can help relieve the symptoms of gastroenteritis, a stomach and bowel infection that can be really uncomfortable. These creams often contain herbs that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm the digestive system. Herbal teas work in a similar way, but by being swallowed and absorbed into the body, rather than applied directly to the skin.  Herbal teas can be very effective in relieving gastroenteritis because they contain active ingredients that can help reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues. For example, ginger (Zingiber officinale) has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach. Turmeric (Curcuma longa) contains a compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce digestive discomfort. Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra) has anti-inflammatory properties and can help protect the stomach lining from irritation.  Drinking herbal teas that contain these herbs can bring several benefits to your life. Firstly, it can help you feel better faster when you're experiencing gastroenteritis symptoms. Secondly, it can help prevent dehydration, which is a common issue when you're experiencing vomiting or diarrhea. Lastly, it can help you maintain a healthy digestive system in the long term, reducing the risk of future stomach and bowel problems.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often a major contributor to gastroenteritis symptoms. Gingerol also has natural antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat bacterial infections that can cause gastroenteritis. The antiemetic properties of Zingiber officinale creams, which are caused by the presence of gingerol, can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with gastroenteritis. The warming properties of Zingiber officinale creams can also help to stimulate digestion and relieve abdominal cramps.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1 tablespoon of grated Zingiber officinale (fresh ginger) root, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of olive oil.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add grated ginger root to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in olive oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool completely before use as a cream for gastroenteritis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is rich in bioactive constituents like curcuminoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract. The curcuminoids in Curcuma longa creams have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to the development of gastroenteritis. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Curcuma longa creams may help soothe the digestive system and promote the healing of the gut lining. The anti-inflammatory properties of Curcuma longa creams can also help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain associated with gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 cups of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a bowl.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax melts.
Add 2 teaspoons of ground turmeric (Curcuma longa) and mix well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory. This compound also contains flavonoids, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritin, that help soothe and calm the digestive system. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, making it an effective treatment for gastroenteritis. Glycyrrhizin also has a protective effect on the stomach lining, preventing further irritation and damage. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as diarrhea, nausea, and stomach pain.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Heat the distilled water in a saucepan over low heat.
Add the glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the water and stir well until the powder dissolves.
In a separate bowl, mix the beeswax, coconut oil, and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Slowly pour the oil mixture into the water mixture and stir constantly until the mixture thickens and becomes creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains quinine, which has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including gastroenteritis. The alkaloids present in Cinchona officinalis, such as quinine and quinidine, have anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. The antipyretic properties of quinine also help lower fever, a common symptom of gastroenteritis. Additionally, quinine has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause gastroenteritis. By reducing inflammation, lowering fever, and fighting off infections, Cinchona officinalis creams can provide relief from the symptoms of gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 2 cups of water. Crush the bark in a coffee grinder or mortar.
Boil the water in a pot. Add the crushed Cinchona bark and let it boil for 10 minutes.
Let the mixture steep for 1 hour. Strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Add 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the strained mixture. Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax melts.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool. Pour it into a tin container and refrigerate until it hardens. Use as a cream for gastroenteritis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains a high concentration of menthol, menthone, and limonene, which are the bioactive constituents responsible for its therapeutic properties. Menthol has a calming effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and soothing digestive discomfort. Menthone helps to relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, easing cramps and spasms associated with gastroenteritis. Limonene has antimicrobial properties, which help to eliminate bacteria and other pathogens that can cause the infection. By reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and eliminating pathogens, Mentha x piperita creams can help to alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of dried Mentha x piperita leaves.
Heat the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until the beeswax melts.
Remove the oil and wax mixture from the heat and add the distilled water. Stir well.
Add the dried Mentha x piperita leaves to the mixture and let it steep for 30 minutes. Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool. Store the Mentha x piperita creams in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin, a mixture of bioactive compounds such as silibinin, silidianin, and silicristin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the gut and protect the digestive system from damage. Silymarin also has a protective effect on liver cells, which is important because liver problems can be a complication of gastroenteritis. By reducing inflammation and protecting the liver, silymarin in Silybum marianum creams may help alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as diarrhea and abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of Silybum marianum flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Dry the Silybum marianum flowers in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours. Let them cool completely.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Add the dried Silybum marianum flowers to the melted oil mixture and stir well. Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool and thicken. Once thickened, stir in the vitamin E oil. Transfer the mixture to a container and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains active constituents like trans-anethole and limonene, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often a symptom of gastroenteritis. The anethole in Foeniculum vulgare creams also has a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate cramps and spasms. The presence of flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, contributes to Foeniculum vulgare creams' ability to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the digestive tract. This combination of properties in Foeniculum vulgare creams makes it a potential treatment option for gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 1 cup of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 2-3 minutes.
Add the dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the heated coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Let the mixture cool and then store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains alkylamides, glycoproteins, and phenolic acids, which are the bioactive constituents that make it effective in treating gastroenteritis. Alkylamides have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Glycoproteins, specifically echinacein, stimulate the immune system to fight off infections, such as those caused by viral gastroenteritis. Phenolic acids have antioxidant properties that help protect the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation, stimulating the immune system, and protecting the digestive tract, Echinacea purpurea creams can help alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until melted.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil and 1 teaspoon of lavender essential oil (optional) to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-aceroside, which are biologically active constituents that help soothe and calm the digestive system. Aloin has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and irritation in the gut, making it easier to recover from gastroenteritis. Aloe-emodin has antibacterial properties that fight off infections that can cause gastroenteritis, while aloe-aceroside has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Aloe barbadensis creams also contain vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like potassium and zinc, which help to nourish and protect the digestive system. By soothing inflammation, fighting off infections, and nourishing the digestive system, aloe barbadensis creams can provide relief from the symptoms of gastroenteritis.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds.
Add shea butter and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir until combined.
Add Aloe barbadensis gel to the mixture and stir until smooth. Pour into a bowl or container.
Let the mixture cool and solidify. Once set, scoop into jars or containers for use. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains boivinoids, including melissic acid, melissoside, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and viral infections in the gut. The antioxidant properties of rosmarinic acid also help protect the gut lining from damage and promote healing. The soothing effects of melissic acid and melissoside can calm digestive issues and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with gastroenteritis. By applying Melissa officinalis creams to the abdomen, you can potentially alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis and support the body's natural healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler until the beeswax is melted.
Add the Melissa officinalis flowers to the melted oil mixture and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the flowers.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before applying it to affected areas.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains sesquiterpene lactones like achillol, achillolide, and alantolactone, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often a problem in gastroenteritis. The cream also contains flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant properties that can protect the digestive system from damage. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Achillea millefolium creams may help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis, such as diarrhea and abdominal pain. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce inflammation and promote the healing of the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for gastroenteritis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Combine the dried Achillea millefolium flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat it over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat. Add the strained mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Achillea millefolium cream is ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gastroenteritis. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, can soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. They may also help calm the digestive system and ease cramps. However, herbal teas don't guarantee prevention, and other factors like hygiene and food safety are also important in preventing gastroenteritis.
faq_safe: While herbal teas may help soothe stomach issues, consuming them daily for gastroenteritis isn't necessarily safe. Some teas can be too strong or interact with medications, making symptoms worse. It's best to have a limited amount occasionally, like when symptoms are mild, to ease discomfort without causing further problems.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with gastroenteritis symptoms like diarrhea and stomach cramps. The effects can be seen within a day or two, but it really depends on the severity of the illness and the type of tea consumed. Some people notice relief from symptoms within 12 to 24 hours after drinking herbal teas regularly.
faq_time: For gastroenteritis, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This helps settle your stomach and calm nausea before meals, making it easier to keep food down. Drinking tea on an empty stomach can also help your body absorb its soothing properties more effectively.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for gastroenteritis. For example, peppermint tea can slow down digestion and worsen nausea, while ginger tea may increase the risk of bleeding if taken with blood thinners. The active compounds in these teas can also affect the way medications work in the body.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have gastroenteritis, it's a good idea to be careful with what you drink. Herbal teas can help soothe your stomach, but some might not be safe for you and your baby. If you're unsure, try sticking to peppermint or chamomile tea, which are commonly used and might be okay. Just be cautious and listen to your body.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a helpful way to soothe gastroenteritis symptoms while breastfeeding. However, some herbs can pass into breast milk, so it's essential to choose ones that are safe during breastfeeding. Peppermint, ginger, and fennel are often considered suitable options, but it's best to check the labels and ingredients carefully to ensure they're free from added ingredients that might not be suitable for you or your baby.
title_2: 11 Herbal Creams to Relieve Gastroenteritis in Food Handlers
